name: Steelcase Leap V2 Chair
introText: The Steelcase Leap V2 has the most adaptable lumbar support of any office chair — its LiveBack flexible back flexes with your spine and is the preferred alternative to the Herman Miller Aeron at $1,299 CAD.

specs.Lumbar: LiveBack flexible support + Lower Back Firmness control

whatWorks: LiveBack technology flexes the entire back support to match your spine as you shift positions — the back bends with you rather than constraining you, reducing fatigue during hours-long sessions.
Lower Back Firmness dial lets you tune lumbar support precisely — unique among ergonomic chairs; you can adjust the resistance of the lumbar support to your exact preference.
worthKnowing: Fabric seat runs warmer than the Aeron's full mesh design — less breathable in hot offices. The Aeron is better for warm climates.
Higher weight capacity (159kg vs. Aeron's 136kg) — the Leap V2 is the better choice for larger users who exceed the Aeron's capacity.

commonComplaints.title: Fabric seat retains heat
commonComplaints.body: The fabric seat and back are less breathable than the Aeron's mesh. Users in warm offices report the Leap running warmer.

commonComplaints.title: Seat depth adjustment is complex
commonComplaints.body: The Leap's seat depth mechanism is more involved than competitors — requires reading the manual to use correctly. Worth learning but not intuitive.

commonComplaints.title: Less aesthetic than Herman Miller
commonComplaints.body: The Leap V2 prioritizes function over form — it looks more industrial than the Aeron's iconic design. Subjective, but notable in design-conscious offices.

verdict: The Steelcase Leap V2 is the best ergonomic chair for users who want maximum lumbar adjustability and can tolerate a fabric seat — the LiveBack and Lower Back Firmness dial are unique. At $500 less than the Aeron, it's the recommended choice for users who need warm-climate breathability from an Aeron.

realBuyers.quote: The lumbar firmness dial is the feature that sold me. Can make it as firm or soft as I need.
Tried both Aeron and Leap. Kept the Leap. The LiveBack system fits my posture better.
12-year warranty, built in America, and I can feel the difference in my back after 8-hour days.

metaDescription: Steelcase Leap V2 review Canada: LiveBack tech, 4D armrests, 12-year warranty. $1,299 CAD. Best lumbar support ergonomic chair for Canadians.
